summary: Bandit security audit completed on all Python source suites (suite-core, suite-api, suite-attack, suite-feeds, suite-evidence-risk, suite-integrations). No HIGH severity findings detected.

medium_severity_summary.detail: SQL injection vectors (B608) detected in 8 locations - all low confidence medium severity flagged due to string-based query construction. Non-parameterized queries detected in detailed_logging.py, feeds_router.py, and feeds_service.py.

low_severity_summary.detail: Low severity findings primarily: Try-Except-Pass patterns (296 instances), hardcoded password strings in enum definitions and environment variable references (140 instances), subprocess calls without shell=True (35 instances, safe).

top_issues_by_category.description: Bare except blocks catching all exceptions without action
String literals containing password/token names (false positives: enum labels, env var names)
SQL queries using f-string concatenation (needs parameterization review)
Subprocess calls without shell=True (safe - no input validation needed)

suite-api/apps/api.detailed_logging.py: 4 issues (1 MEDIUM SQL, 2 LOW try_except_pass, 1 LOW hardcoded_string)

notes: No HIGH severity vulnerabilities detected in 161,207 lines of Python code
SQL injection findings are LOW-MEDIUM confidence and limited to 8 locations - all require review for parameterization
Hardcoded password findings are primarily false positives: enum class attribute names, enum values, and environment variable name strings
Try-Except-Pass patterns (296 instances) are design choices in exception handling but represent best-practice gaps
Subprocess calls are safe (no shell=True, hardcoded commands)

remediation_priority.medium: Review 8 SQL injection flags in feeds_router.py and feeds_service.py for parameterization
remediation_priority.low: Replace try-except-pass with specific exception handling or logging; review hardcoded string false positives
